cards.prompt: Who was the first Roman emperor?
cards.long_answer: Augustus (Gaius Octavius), who took power in 27 BC after the fall of the Republic and reigned until 14 AD.
Tiberius, Augustus's stepson, ruled 14–37 AD. He retired to Capri in his later years.

cards.prompt: Which emperor reportedly fiddled while Rome burned?
cards.long_answer: Nero, ruling 54–68 AD. The 'fiddling' story is largely a later legend, but the great fire of 64 AD did happen during his reign.
Marcus Aurelius (161–180 AD), author of the Meditations. He is the last of the so-called 'Five Good Emperors'.

cards.prompt: Which emperor split the Roman Empire administratively into East and West?
cards.long_answer: Diocletian, in 285 AD, established the tetrarchy: two senior Augusti and two junior Caesars governing different regions.

cards.prompt: Which emperor legalised Christianity in the empire?
cards.long_answer: Constantine the Great, via the Edict of Milan in 313 AD, which granted religious tolerance throughout the empire.
